originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&irq` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE true)
     (= CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN true)
     (= CONFIG_OF_IRQ false))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_OF_IRQ () Bool
  false)
reasoning: The known VBDB bug for 6252547b8a7.c is a core.NullDereference at L12 (PRODUCT_23/TRUE: null deref of ops when OF_IRQ=F). This alarm is DEAD_STORE for irq at L12 (int irq = *ops, but irq is never subsequently read) — a different, new bug. TWL4030=T internally #defines CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN (L4), making the explicit IRQ_DOMAIN=T in the PC redundant. The dead store exists for all TWL4030=T configs regardless of OF_IRQ: when OF_IRQ=T ops is valid and irq is still never read; when OF_IRQ=F a null deref occurs but irq is still dead. The PC unnecessarily includes OF_IRQ=F, missing OF_IRQ=T configs where the dead store also occurs. The true condition is TWL4030=T. Over-constrained (too narrow).
